content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

November 7, 2011

Anita D. Stearns Mayo
Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman, LLP
P O Box 7880
San Francisco, CA 94120-7880

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our file No. W-11-190

Dear Ms. Stearns Mayo:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the lobbying provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Your letter asks about and analyzes your situation under the recent Assembly Bill 1743 ("AB 1743"), effective as of January 1, 2011. On October 9, Governor Jerry Brown signed into law Senate Bill 398 ("SB 398"), effective on that date. Because SB 398 changes and updates several provisions of AB 1743 on which your request for advice relies, we are withdrawing your request. Should you have further questions after applying the now-effective SB 398, please feel free to contact us at that time.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Zackery P. Morazzini
General Counsel

[signature: Heather M Rowan]

By: Heather M. Rowan
Counsel, Legal Division

HMR:jgl

___________________________

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

The requestor is invited to resubmit questions after applying the now-effective SB 398.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Withdrawal letter with no explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The legal question and conclusion were synthesized from the body of the letter. File reference W-11-190 indicates this is a withdrawal matter.

The requestor is invited to resubmit questions after applying the now-effective SB 398.

embedding.summary: The FPPC withdraws an advice request regarding lobbying provisions under AB 1743 due to the October 9, 2011 enactment of SB 398, which supersedes key provisions relied upon in the original request.
